inputs.prompt: Author: James Chen, CEO, Clearwater Analytics. Argument: Local government finance departments are making critical decisions with outdated tools. The private sector invested in AI forecasting years ago. Public money deserves at least the same level of analytical capability. Target publication: Public Finance Magazine (trade press for government finance professionals). Evidence: 1) Only 12% of local authorities use any form of AI in financial planning (internal survey). 2) £2.1 billion in local government overspend in the last fiscal year attributed to forecasting errors (NAO report). News hook: Government has just announced a new 'digital transformation' fund for local authorities.

Author: Helen Cross, Director, Riverside Arts Centre. Argument: Arts funding should not be treated as a luxury. Target publication: Local newspaper. Evidence: The arts centre brings £1.2M to the local economy annually.

Author: Dr. Ingrid Nørgaard, Executive Director, Nordic Health Initiative. Argument: Europe's healthcare systems are structured nationally but the diseases they fight do not respect borders. Cross-border healthcare research is not an aspiration — it is a necessity, and the EU must fund it as infrastructure, not as project grants. Target publication: Politico Europe. Evidence: 1) Nordic cross-border research collaborations produced 23% more published findings per euro invested than single-country projects (NHI internal data). 2) The EU4Health programme allocates less than 4% of its budget to cross-border research. News hook: EU budget negotiations for 2028-2034 are beginning.
